mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.09% is near zero, indicating an accommodative monetary stance. Sustained low FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPIAUCSL (236.254) signals inflation persistence despite monetary ease, forcing the Fed to signal future tightening. Market expectations of rate increases drive 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered instances over 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.05 when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 6.3% is elevated relative to historical lows, indicating labor market slack. Persistent slack suppresses wage growth expectations and inflation expectations, causing the market to price in lower long-term real rates. This downward pressure on 10-year Treasury yields persists over the next 20 trading days as labor market recovery remains incomplete.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 20-day forward periods when UNRATE > 6.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 434,571 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 946 thousand indicate moderate demand. When both series decline month-over-month by more than 2%, it signals weakening aggregate demand and recession risk. Market reprices long-duration assets downward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors flee to safety over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% and HOUST declines > 2% in same month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) at 102.7435 is near trend. Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO above 0.5% signals supply-side expansion and productivity gains, reducing inflation pressure and allowing the Fed to maintain accommodative policy. This supports lower long-term real rates and depresses 10-year Treasury yields over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth-without-inflation expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in < 45% of triggered instances over 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) at 138,252 thousand shows solid labor market conditions. When PAYEMS growth accelerates above 200k per month while UNRATE remains stable or declines, it signals tight labor markets and wage pressure. Market expectations shift toward Fed tightening, driving 10-year Treasury yields upward over the next 60 trading days as the terminal rate reprices higher.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200k and UNRATE declines or remains flat
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage acceleration
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered instances over 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 15,946.6 billion (chained 2017 dollars) represents the current demand level. When quarterly GDP growth decelerates below 1.5% annualized rate, it signals weakening economic momentum. Market reprices growth expectations downward and reduces inflation expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall over the next 60 trading days as investors demand lower compensation for duration risk.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 60-day forward periods when GDP growth < 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% and CPIAUCSL shows year-over-year growth above 3%, the real federal funds rate is deeply negative. This regime of financial repression incentivizes investors to demand higher nominal yields on long-duration assets. 10-year Treasury yields rise over the next 20 trading days as the market prices in eventual normalization of real rates.
regime_hint: negative real rates with inflation above target
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) at 946 thousand represent residential investment demand. When HOUST rises above 1,000 thousand units annualized for two consecutive months, it signals strong housing demand and forward-looking economic confidence. This demand signal compresses long-term real rates as growth expectations rise, but simultaneously increases inflation expectations, producing ambiguous yield direction. The net effect is positive 10-year Treasury yields over the next 5 trading days as inflation expectations dominate.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,000 thousand for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered instances over 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04.
